what: Interval Wise Testing for Functional Data
title: Interval Wise Testing for Functional Data
text: goal fdatest implement various statistical methods domain selection functional data analysis, selecting subset domain difference two populations significant. package based paper Abramowicz et al. (2022) Pini & Vantini (2017).

title: Interval Wise Testing for Functional Data
text: can install package CRAN : Alternatively, can install development version fdatest GitHub :
code: install.packages("fdatest") # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("permaverse/fdatest")

title: Interval Wise Testing for Functional Data
text: package provides several methods domain selection, including: FDR2: False Discovery Rate two populations. Global2: Global testing two populations. IWT2: Interval-Wise Testing two populations. PCT2: Partition Closed Testing two populations. TWT2: Threshold Wise Testing two populations. can use methods test significant differences two populations functional data. example using TWT2 method NASA temperatures data set:
code: library(fdatest)  # Performing the TWT for two populations on the NASA temperatures data set withr::with_seed(1234, {   out <- TWT2(NASAtemp$paris, NASAtemp$milan) })  # Plotting the results of the TWT plot(   out,   xrange = c(0, 12),   main = 'TWT results for testing mean differences' )

what: Two population functional Benjamini-Hochberg procedure — FDR2
title: Two population functional Benjamini-Hochberg procedure — FDR2
text: function implements functional Benjamini Hochberg (fBH) procedure testing mean differences two functional populations. Functional data tested locally unadjusted adjusted p-value functions provided. unadjusted p-value function controls point-wise error rate. adjusted p-value function controls family-wise error rate asymptotically.

title: Two population functional Benjamini-Hochberg procedure — FDR2
code: FDR2(   data1,   data2,   mu = 0,   dx = NULL,   B = 1000L,   paired = FALSE,   alternative = c("two.sided", "less", "greater") )  fdr2(   data1,   data2,   mu = 0,   dx = NULL,   n_perm = 1000L,   paired = FALSE,   alternative = c("two.sided", "less", "greater") )

title: Two population functional Benjamini-Hochberg procedure — FDR2
text: data1 Either numeric matrix object class fda::fd specifying data first sample. data provided within matrix, shape \(n_1 \times J\) contain row one \(n_1\) functions sample columns evaluation function uniform grid size \(J\). data2 Either numeric matrix object class fda::fd specifying data second sample. data provided within matrix, shape \(n_2 \times J\) contain row one \(n_2\) functions sample columns evaluation function uniform grid size \(J\). mu Either numeric value numeric vector object class fda::fd specifying functional mean difference null hypothesis. mu constant, constant function used. mu numeric vector, must correspond evaluation mean difference function grid used evaluate data samples. Defaults 0. dx numeric value specifying discretization step grid used evaluate functional data provided objects class fda::fd. Defaults NULL, case default value 0.01 used corresponds grid size 100L. Unused functional data provided form matrices. B integer value specifying number iterations MC algorithm evaluate p-value permutation tests. Defaults 1000L. paired boolean value specifying whether paired test performed. Defaults FALSE. alternative string specifying type alternative hypothesis. Choices "two.sided", "less" "greater". Defaults "two.sided". n_perm integer value specifying number permutations permutation tests. Defaults 1000L.

title: Two population functional Benjamini-Hochberg procedure — FDR2
text: object class ftwosample containing following components: data: numeric matrix shape \(n \times J\) containing evaluation \(n = n_1 + n_2\) functions common uniform grid size \(p\). group_labels: integer vector size \(n = n_1 + n_2\) containing group membership function. mu: numeric vector shape \(J\) containing evaluation functional mean difference null hypothesis uniform grid used evaluate functional samples. unadjusted_pvalues: numeric vector size \(J\) containing evaluation unadjusted p-value function uniform grid used evaluate functional samples. adjusted_pvalues: numeric vector size \(J\) containing evaluation adjusted p-value functione uniform grid used evaluate functional samples. Optionally, list may contain following components: global_pvalue: numeric value containing global p-value. present correction argument set "Global". pvalue_matrix: numeric matrix shape \(p \times p\) containing p-values interval-wise tests. Element \(, j\) contains p-value test performed interval indexed \(j, j+1 , \dots, j+(p-)\). present correction argument set "IWT".

what: Two population Global Testing procedure — Global2
title: Two population Global Testing procedure — Global2
text: function implements Global Testing procedure testing mean differences two functional populations. Functional data tested locally unadjusted adjusted p-value functions provided. unadjusted p-value function controls point-wise error rate. adjusted p-value function controls interval-wise error rate.

title: Two population Global Testing procedure — Global2
code: Global2(   data1,   data2,   mu = 0,   dx = NULL,   B = 1000L,   paired = FALSE,   statistic = c("Integral", "Max", "Integral_std", "Max_std") )  global2(   data1,   data2,   mu = 0,   dx = NULL,   n_perm = 1000L,   paired = FALSE,   statistic = c("Integral", "Max", "Integral_std", "Max_std") )

title: Two population Global Testing procedure — Global2
text: data1 Either numeric matrix object class fda::fd specifying data first sample. data provided within matrix, shape \(n_1 \times J\) contain row one \(n_1\) functions sample columns evaluation function uniform grid size \(J\). data2 Either numeric matrix object class fda::fd specifying data second sample. data provided within matrix, shape \(n_2 \times J\) contain row one \(n_2\) functions sample columns evaluation function uniform grid size \(J\). mu Either numeric value numeric vector object class fda::fd specifying functional mean difference null hypothesis. mu constant, constant function used. mu numeric vector, must correspond evaluation mean difference function grid used evaluate data samples. Defaults 0. dx numeric value specifying discretization step grid used evaluate functional data provided objects class fda::fd. Defaults NULL, case default value 0.01 used corresponds grid size 100L. Unused functional data provided form matrices. B integer value specifying number iterations MC algorithm evaluate p-value permutation tests. Defaults 1000L. paired boolean value specifying whether paired test performed. Defaults FALSE. statistic string specifying test statistic use. "Max_std": Maximum squared t-test statistic. Defaults "Integral". n_perm integer value specifying number permutations permutation tests. Defaults 1000L.

title: Two population Global Testing procedure — Global2

Optionally, list may contain following components: global_pvalue: numeric value containing global p-value. present correction argument set "Global". pvalue_matrix: numeric matrix shape \(p \times p\) containing p-values interval-wise tests. what: Global testing procedure for testing functional-on-scalar linear models — Globallm
title: Global testing procedure for testing functional-on-scalar linear models — Globallm
text: function used fit test functional linear models. can used carry regression, analysis variance. implements global testing procedure testing significance effects scalar covariates functional population.

title: Global testing procedure for testing functional-on-scalar linear models — Globallm
code: Globallm(   formula,   dx = NULL,   B = 1000L,   method = c("residuals", "responses"),   stat = c("Integral", "Max") )  global_lm(   formula,   dx = NULL,   n_perm = 1000L,   method = c("residuals", "responses"),   stat = c("Integral", "Max") )

title: Global testing procedure for testing functional-on-scalar linear models — Globallm
text: formula object class stats::formula (one can coerced class) specifying model fitted symbolic fashion. output variable (left-hand side) formula can either matrix dimension \(n \times J\) containing pointwise evaluations \(n\) functions grid \(J\) points, object class fda::fd. dx numeric value specifying discretization step grid used evaluate functional data provided objects class fda::fd. Defaults NULL, case default value 0.01 used corresponds grid size 100L. Unused functional data provided form matrices. B integer value specifying number iterations MC algorithm evaluate p-value permutation tests. Defaults 1000L. method string specifying method used calculate p-value permutation tests. Choices either "residuals" performs permutation residuals reduced model according Freedman Lane scheme "responses", performs permutation responses, according Manly scheme. Defaults "residuals". stat string specifying test statistic used global test. Choices either "Integral", case statistic defined integral F-test statistic domain, "Max", case statistic defined maximum F-test statistic domain. Defaults  "Integral". n_perm integer value specifying number permutations permutation tests. Defaults 1000L.

title: Global testing procedure for testing functional-on-scalar linear models — Globallm
text: object class flm containing following components: call: matched call. design_matrix: design matrix functional--scalar linear model. unadjusted_pval_F: Evaluation grid unadjusted p-value function functional F-test. adjusted_pval_F: Evaluation grid adjusted p-value function functional F-test. unadjusted_pval_part: Evaluation grid unadjusted p-value function functional F-tests factor analysis variance (rows). adjusted_pval_part: Adjusted p-values functional F-tests factor analysis variance (rows) basis coefficient (columns). data_eval: Evaluation fine uniform grid functional data obtained basis expansion. coeff_regr_eval: Evaluation fine uniform grid functional regression coefficients. fitted_eval: Evaluation fine uniform grid fitted values functional regression. residuals_eval: Evaluation fine uniform grid residuals functional regression. R2_eval: Evaluation fine uniform grid functional R-squared regression. Optionally, list may contain following components: pval_matrix_F: Matrix dimensions c(p,p) p-values intervalwise F-tests. element \((,j)\) matrix pval_matrix contains p-value test interval indexed \((j,j+1,...,j+(p-))\); component present correction set "IWT". pval_matrix_part: Array dimensions c(L+1,p,p) p-values multivariate F-tests factors. element \((l,,j)\) array pval_matrix_part contains p-value joint NPC test factor l components \((j,j+1,...,j+(p-))\); component present correction set "IWT". Global_pval_F: Global p-value overall test F; component present correction set "Global". Global_pval_part: Global p-value test F involving factor separately; component present correction set "Global".

text: Abramowicz, K., Pini, ., Schelin, L., Stamm, ., & Vantini, S. (2022). “Domain selection familywise error rate functional data: unified framework. Biometrics 79(2), 1119-1132. D. Freedman D. Lane (1983). Nonstochastic Interpretation Reported Significance Levels. Journal Business & Economic Statistics 1(4), 292-298. B. F. J. Manly (2006). Randomization, Bootstrap Monte Carlo Methods Biology. Vol. 70. CRC Press.

title: Global testing procedure for testing functional-on-scalar linear models — Globallm
code: # Defining the covariates temperature <- rbind(NASAtemp$milan, NASAtemp$paris) groups <- c(rep(0, 22), rep(1, 22))  # Performing the IWT Global_result <- Globallm(temperature ~ groups, B = 1000) #>  #> ── Point-wise tests ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  #> ── Global test ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  #> ── Global Testing completed ──────────────────────────────────────────────────── # Summary of the IWT results summary(Global_result) #> $call #> global_lm(formula = formula, dx = dx, n_perm = B, method = method,  #>     stat = stat) #>  #> $ttest #>             Minimum p-value     #> (Intercept)               0 *** #> groups                    0 *** #>  #> $R2 #>               Range of functional R-squared #> Min R-squared                  3.390203e-05 #> Max R-squared                  5.399620e-01 #>  #> $ftest #>   Minimum p-value     #> 1               0 *** #>   # Plot of the IWT results plot(   Global_result,   main = 'NASA data',   plot_adjpval = TRUE,   xlab = 'Day',   xrange = c(1, 365) )   plot(   Global_result,   main = 'NASA data',   plot_adjpval = TRUE,   xlab = 'Day',   xrange = c(1, 365) )

what: Interval Testing Procedure for testing unctional analysis of variance with B-spline basis — ITPaovbspline
title: Interval Testing Procedure for testing unctional analysis of variance with B-spline basis — ITPaovbspline
text: function implements Interval Testing Procedure testing significant differences several functional population evaluated uniform grid, functional analysis variance setting. Data represented means B-spline basis significance basis coefficient tested interval-wise control Family Wise Error Rate. default parameters basis expansion lead piece-wise interpolating function.

title: Interval Testing Procedure for testing unctional analysis of variance with B-spline basis — ITPaovbspline
code: ITPaovbspline(   formula,   order = 2,   nknots = dim(stats::model.response(stats::model.frame(formula)))[2],   B = 1000,   method = "residuals" )

title: Interval Testing Procedure for testing unctional analysis of variance with B-spline basis — ITPaovbspline
text: formula object class "formula" (one can coerced class): symbolic description model fitted. order Order B-spline basis expansion. default order=2. nknots Number knots B-spline basis expansion. default nknots=dim(data1)[2]. B number iterations MC algorithm evaluate p-values permutation tests. defualt B=1000. method Permutation method used calculate p-value permutation tests. Choose "residuals" permutations residuals reduced model, according Freedman Lane scheme, "responses" permutation responses, according Manly scheme.

title: Interval Testing Procedure for testing unctional analysis of variance with B-spline basis — ITPaovbspline
text: ITPaovbspline returns object class "ITPaov". function summary used obtain print summary results. object class "ITPaov" list containing least following components: call matched call. design_matrix design matrix functional--scalar linear model. basis String vector indicating basis used first phase algorithm. case equal "B-spline". coeff Matrix dimensions c(n,p) p coefficients B-spline basis expansion. Rows associated units columns basis index. coeff_regr Matrix dimensions c(L+1,p) p coefficients B-spline basis expansion intercept (first row) L effects covariates specified formula. Columns associated basis index. pval_F Unadjusted p-values functional F-test basis coefficient. pval_matrix_F Matrix dimensions c(p,p) p-values multivariate F-tests. element (,j) matrix pval_matrix contains p-value joint NPC test components (j,j+1,...,j+(p-)). adjusted_pval_F Adjusted p-values functional F-test basis coefficient. pval_factors Unadjusted p-values functional F-tests factor analysis variance, separately (rows) basis coefficient (columns). pval_matrix_factors Array dimensions c(L+1,p,p) p-values multivariate F-tests factors. element (l,,j) array pval_matrix contains p-value joint NPC test factor l components (j,j+1,...,j+(p-)). adjusted_pval_factors adjusted p-values functional F-tests factor analysis variance (rows) basis coefficient (columns). data_eval Evaluation fine uniform grid functional data obtained basis expansion. coeff_regr_eval Evaluation fine uniform grid functional regression coefficients. fitted_eval Evaluation fine uniform grid fitted values functional regression. residuals_eval Evaluation fine uniform grid residuals functional regression. R2_eval Evaluation fine uniform grid functional R-squared regression. heatmap_matrix_F Heatmap matrix p-values functional F-test (used plots). heatmap_matrix_factors Heatmap matrix p-values functional F-tests factor analysis variance (used plots).

what: Interval Testing Procedure for testing Functional-on-Scalar Linear Models with B-spline basis — ITPlmbspline
title: Interval Testing Procedure for testing Functional-on-Scalar Linear Models with B-spline basis — ITPlmbspline
text: function used fit test functional linear models. can used carry regression, analysis variance. implements Interval Testing Procedure testing significance effects scalar covariates functional population evaluated uniform grid. Data represented means B-spline basis significance basis coefficient tested interval-wise control Family Wise Error Rate. default parameters basis expansion lead piece-wise interpolating function.

title: Interval Testing Procedure for testing Functional-on-Scalar Linear Models with B-spline basis — ITPlmbspline
code: ITPlmbspline(   formula,   order = 2,   nknots = dim(stats::model.response(stats::model.frame(formula)))[2],   B = 10000,   method = "residuals" )

title: Interval Testing Procedure for testing Functional-on-Scalar Linear Models with B-spline basis — ITPlmbspline
text: formula object class "formula" (one can coerced class): symbolic description model fitted. order Order B-spline basis expansion. default order=2. nknots Number knots B-spline basis expansion. default nknots=dim(data1)[2]. B number iterations MC algorithm evaluate p-values permutation tests. defualt B=1000. method Permutation method used calculate p-value permutation tests. Choose "residuals" permutations residuals reduced model, according Freedman Lane scheme, "responses" permutation responses, according Manly scheme.

title: Interval Testing Procedure for testing Functional-on-Scalar Linear Models with B-spline basis — ITPlmbspline
text: ITPlmbspline returns object class "ITPlm". function summary used obtain print summary results. object class "ITPlm" list containing least following components: call matched call. design_matrix design matrix functional--scalar linear model. basis String vector indicating basis used first phase algorithm. case equal "B-spline". coeff Matrix dimensions c(n,p) p coefficients B-spline basis expansion. Rows associated units columns basis index. coeff_regr Matrix dimensions c(L+1,p) p coefficients B-spline basis expansion intercept (first row) L effects covariates specified formula. Columns associated basis index. pval_F Unadjusted p-values functional F-test basis coefficient. pval_matrix_F Matrix dimensions c(p,p) p-values multivariate F-tests. element (,j) matrix pval_matrix contains p-value joint NPC test components (j,j+1,...,j+(p-)). adjusted_pval_F Adjusted p-values functional F-test basis coefficient. pval_t Unadjusted p-values functional t-tests partial regression coefficient including intercept (rows) basis coefficient (columns). pval_matrix_t Array dimensions c(L+1,p,p) p-values multivariate t-tests. element (l,,j) array pval_matrix contains p-value joint NPC test covariate l components (j,j+1,...,j+(p-)). adjusted_pval_t adjusted p-values functional t-tests partial regression coefficient including intercept (rows) basis coefficient (columns). data_eval Evaluation fine uniform grid functional data obtained basis expansion. coeff_regr_eval Evaluation fine uniform grid functional regression coefficients. fitted_eval Evaluation fine uniform grid fitted values functional regression. residuals_eval Evaluation fine uniform grid residuals functional regression. R2_eval Evaluation fine uniform grid functional R-squared regression. heatmap_matrix_F Heatmap matrix p-values functional F-test (used plots). heatmap_matrix_t Heatmap matrix p-values functional t-tests (used plots).

what: One population Interval Wise Testing procedure — ITP1bspline
title: One population Interval Wise Testing procedure — ITP1bspline
text: function implements Interval Wise Testing procedure testing center symmetry functional population. Functional data tested locally  unadjusted  adjusted p-value functions provided. unadjusted p-value function controls point-wise error rate. adjusted p-value function controls interval-wise error rate.

title: One population Interval Wise Testing procedure — ITP1bspline
code: ITP1bspline(data, mu = 0, B = 1000, order = 2, nknots = dim(data)[2])  ITP1fourier(data, mu = 0, B = 1000, maxfrequency = floor(dim(data)[2]/2))  IWT1(data, mu = 0, B = 1000L, dx = NULL, recycle = TRUE)  iwt1(data, mu = 0, n_perm = 1000L, dx = NULL, recycle = TRUE)

title: One population Interval Wise Testing procedure — ITP1bspline
text: data Either pointwise evaluations functional data set uniform grid, fd. pointwise evaluations provided, data matrix dimensions c(n, J), J evaluations columns n units rows. mu center symmetry null hypothesis. Three possibilities available mu: constant (case, constant function used); J-dimensional vector containing evaluations grid data evaluated; fd object containing one function. Defaults 0. B number iterations MC algorithm evaluate p-values permutation tests. Defaults 1000L. order Order B-spline basis expansion. Defaults 2L. nknots Number knots B-spline basis expansion. Defaults dim(data)[2]. maxfrequency maximum frequency used Fourier basis expansion data. Defaults floor(dim(data)[2] / 2), leading interpolating expansion. dx Used fd object provided. case, dx size discretization step gridused evaluate functional data. set NULL, grid size 100L used. Defaults NULL. recycle Flag used decide whether recycled version IWT used (see Pini Vantini, 2017 details). Defaults TRUE. n_perm integer value specifying number permutations permutation tests. Defaults 1000L.

title: One population Interval Wise Testing procedure — ITP1bspline
text: object class IWT1, list containing least following components: test: String vector indicating type test performed. case equal "1pop". mu: Evaluation grid center symmetry null hypothesis (entered user). unadjusted_pval: Evaluation grid unadjusted p-value function. pval_matrix: Matrix dimensions c(p, p) p-values multivariate tests. element (,j) matrix pval_matrix contains p-value joint NPC test components (j,j+1,...,j+(p-)). adjusted_pval: Evaluation grid adjusted p-value function. data_eval: Evaluation grid functional data.

what: Two population Interval Wise Testing procedure — ITP2bspline
title: Two population Interval Wise Testing procedure — ITP2bspline
text: function implements Interval Wise Testing procedure testing mean differences two functional populations. Functional data tested locally unadjusted adjusted p-value functions provided. unadjusted p-value function controls point-wise error rate. adjusted p-value function controls interval-wise error rate.

title: Two population Interval Wise Testing procedure — ITP2bspline
code: ITP2bspline(   data1,   data2,   mu = 0,   B = 1000,   paired = FALSE,   order = 2,   nknots = dim(data1)[2] )  ITP2fourier(   data1,   data2,   mu = 0,   B = 1000,   paired = FALSE,   maxfrequency = floor(dim(data1)[2]/2) )  ITP2pafourier(   data1,   data2,   mu = 0,   B = 1000,   paired = FALSE,   maxfrequency = floor(dim(data1)[2]/2) )  IWT2(   data1,   data2,   mu = 0,   dx = NULL,   B = 1000L,   paired = FALSE,   alternative = c("two.sided", "less", "greater"),   verbose = FALSE,   recycle = TRUE )  iwt2(   data1,   data2,   mu = 0,   dx = NULL,   n_perm = 1000L,   paired = FALSE,   alternative = c("two.sided", "less", "greater"),   verbose = FALSE,   recycle = TRUE )

title: Two population Interval Wise Testing procedure — ITP2bspline
text: data1 Either numeric matrix object class fda::fd specifying data first sample. data provided within matrix, shape \(n_1 \times J\) contain row one \(n_1\) functions sample columns evaluation function uniform grid size \(J\). data2 Either numeric matrix object class fda::fd specifying data second sample. data provided within matrix, shape \(n_2 \times J\) contain row one \(n_2\) functions sample columns evaluation function uniform grid size \(J\). mu Either numeric value numeric vector object class fda::fd specifying functional mean difference null hypothesis. mu constant, constant function used. mu numeric vector, must correspond evaluation mean difference function grid used evaluate data samples. Defaults 0. B integer value specifying number iterations MC algorithm evaluate p-value permutation tests. Defaults 1000L. paired boolean value specifying whether paired test performed. Defaults FALSE. order Order B-spline basis expansion. Defaults 2L. nknots Number knots B-spline basis expansion. Defaults dim(data1)[2]. maxfrequency maximum frequency used Fourier basis expansion data. Defaults floor(dim(data1)[2] / 2), leading interpolating expansion. dx numeric value specifying discretization step grid used evaluate functional data provided objects class fda::fd. Defaults NULL, case default value 0.01 used corresponds grid size 100L. Unused functional data provided form matrices. alternative string specifying type alternative hypothesis. Choices "two.sided", "less" "greater". Defaults "two.sided". verbose boolean value specifying whether print progress computation. Defaults FALSE. recycle boolean value specifying whether recycled version interval-wise testing procedure used. See Pini Vantini (2017) details. Defaults TRUE. n_perm integer value specifying number permutations permutation tests. Defaults 1000L.

title: Two population Interval Wise Testing procedure — ITP2bspline
text: object class ftwosample containing following components: data: numeric matrix shape \(n \times J\) containing evaluation \(n = n_1 + n_2\) functions common uniform grid size \(p\). group_labels: integer vector size \(n = n_1 + n_2\) containing group membership function. mu: numeric vector shape \(J\) containing evaluation functional mean difference null hypothesis uniform grid used evaluate functional samples. unadjusted_pvalues: numeric vector size \(J\) containing evaluation unadjusted p-value function uniform grid used evaluate functional samples. adjusted_pvalues: numeric vector size \(J\) containing evaluation adjusted p-value functione uniform grid used evaluate functional samples. Optionally, list may contain following components: global_pvalue: numeric value containing global p-value. present correction argument set "Global". pvalue_matrix: numeric matrix shape \(p \times p\) containing p-values interval-wise tests. Element \(, j\) contains p-value test performed interval indexed \(j, j+1 , \dots, j+(p-)\). present correction argument set "IWT".

title: Interval Wise Testing procedure for testing functional analysis of variance — IWTaov

Functional data tested locally unadjusted adjusted p-value functions provided. unadjusted p-value function controls point-wise error rate. adjusted p-value function controls interval-wise error rate.

title: Interval Wise Testing procedure for testing functional analysis of variance — IWTaov
code: IWTaov(   formula,   dx = NULL,   B = 1000L,   method = c("residuals", "responses"),   recycle = TRUE )  iwt_aov(   formula,   dx = NULL,   n_perm = 1000L,   method = c("residuals", "responses"),   recycle = TRUE )

title: Interval Wise Testing procedure for testing functional analysis of variance — IWTaov
text: formula object class stats::formula (one can coerced class) specifying model fitted symbolic fashion. output variable (left-hand side) formula can either matrix dimension \(n \times J\) containing pointwise evaluations \(n\) functions grid \(J\) points, object class fda::fd. dx numeric value specifying discretization step grid used evaluate functional data provided objects class fda::fd. Defaults NULL, case default value 0.01 used corresponds grid size 100L. Unused functional data provided form matrices. B integer value specifying number iterations MC algorithm evaluate p-value permutation tests. Defaults 1000L. method string specifying method used calculate p-value permutation tests. Choices either "residuals" performs permutation residuals reduced model according Freedman Lane scheme "responses", performs permutation responses, according Manly scheme. Defaults "residuals". recycle boolean value specifying whether recycled version interval-wise testing procedure used. See Pini Vantini (2017) details. Defaults TRUE. n_perm integer value specifying number permutations permutation tests. Defaults 1000L.

title: Interval Wise Testing procedure for testing functional analysis of variance — IWTaov
text: object class fanova containing following components: call: matched call. design_matrix: design matrix functional--scalar linear model. unadjusted_pval_F: Evaluation grid unadjusted p-value function functional F-test. adjusted_pval_F: Evaluation grid adjusted p-value function functional F-test. unadjusted_pval_factors: Evaluation grid unadjusted p-value function functional F-tests factor analysis variance (rows). adjusted_pval_factors: Adjusted p-values functional F-tests factor analysis variance (rows) basis coefficient (columns). data_eval: Evaluation fine uniform grid functional data obtained basis expansion. coeff_regr_eval: Evaluation fine uniform grid functional regression coefficients. fitted_eval: Evaluation fine uniform grid fitted values functional regression. residuals_eval: Evaluation fine uniform grid residuals functional regression. R2_eval: Evaluation fine uniform grid functional R-squared regression. Optionally, list may contain following components: pval_matrix_F: Matrix dimensions c(p,p) p-values intervalwise F-tests. element \((,j)\) matrix pval_matrix contains p-value test interval indexed \((j,j+1,...,j+(p-))\); component present correction set "IWT". pval_matrix_factors: Array dimensions c(L+1,p,p) p-values multivariate F-tests factors. element \((l,,j)\) array pval_matrix contains p-value joint NPC test factor l components \((j,j+1,...,j+(p-))\); component present correction set "IWT". heatmap_matrix_F: Heatmap matrix p-values functional F-test (used plots); component present correction set "IWT". heatmap_matrix_factors: Heatmap matrix p-values functional F-tests factor analysis variance (used plots); component present correction set "IWT". Global_pval_F: Global p-value overall test F; component present correction set "Global". Global_pval_factors: Global p-value test F involving factor separately; component present correction set "Global".

what: Interval-wise testing procedure for testing functional-on-scalar linear models — IWTlm
title: Interval-wise testing procedure for testing functional-on-scalar linear models — IWTlm
text: function used fit test functional linear models. can used carry regression, analysis variance. implements interval-wise testing procedure (IWT) testing significance effects scalar covariates functional population.

title: Interval-wise testing procedure for testing functional-on-scalar linear models — IWTlm
code: IWTlm(   formula,   dx = NULL,   B = 1000L,   method = c("residuals", "responses"),   recycle = TRUE )  iwt_lm(   formula,   dx = NULL,   n_perm = 1000L,   method = c("residuals", "responses"),   recycle = TRUE )

title: Interval-wise testing procedure for testing functional-on-scalar linear models — IWTlm
text: formula object class stats::formula (one can coerced class) specifying model fitted symbolic fashion. output variable (left-hand side) formula can either matrix dimension \(n \times J\) containing pointwise evaluations \(n\) functions grid \(J\) points, object class fda::fd. dx numeric value specifying discretization step grid used evaluate functional data provided objects class fda::fd. Defaults NULL, case default value 0.01 used corresponds grid size 100L. Unused functional data provided form matrices. B integer value specifying number iterations MC algorithm evaluate p-value permutation tests. Defaults 1000L. method string specifying method used calculate p-value permutation tests. Choices either "residuals" performs permutation residuals reduced model according Freedman Lane scheme "responses", performs permutation responses, according Manly scheme. Defaults "residuals". recycle boolean value specifying whether recycled version interval-wise testing procedure used. See Pini Vantini (2017) details. Defaults TRUE. n_perm integer value specifying number permutations permutation tests. Defaults 1000L.

title: Interval-wise testing procedure for testing functional-on-scalar linear models — IWTlm
text: object class flm containing following components: call: matched call. design_matrix: design matrix functional--scalar linear model. unadjusted_pval_F: Evaluation grid unadjusted p-value function functional F-test. adjusted_pval_F: Evaluation grid adjusted p-value function functional F-test. unadjusted_pval_part: Evaluation grid unadjusted p-value function functional F-tests factor analysis variance (rows). adjusted_pval_part: Adjusted p-values functional F-tests factor analysis variance (rows) basis coefficient (columns). data_eval: Evaluation fine uniform grid functional data obtained basis expansion. coeff_regr_eval: Evaluation fine uniform grid functional regression coefficients. fitted_eval: Evaluation fine uniform grid fitted values functional regression. residuals_eval: Evaluation fine uniform grid residuals functional regression. R2_eval: Evaluation fine uniform grid functional R-squared regression. Optionally, list may contain following components: pval_matrix_F: Matrix dimensions c(p,p) p-values intervalwise F-tests. element \((,j)\) matrix pval_matrix contains p-value test interval indexed \((j,j+1,...,j+(p-))\); component present correction set "IWT". pval_matrix_part: Array dimensions c(L+1,p,p) p-values multivariate F-tests factors. element \((l,,j)\) array pval_matrix_part contains p-value joint NPC test factor l components \((j,j+1,...,j+(p-))\); component present correction set "IWT". Global_pval_F: Global p-value overall test F; component present correction set "Global". Global_pval_part: Global p-value test F involving factor separately; component present correction set "Global".

what: Two population Partition Closed Testing procedure — PCT2
title: Two population Partition Closed Testing procedure — PCT2
text: function implements Partition Closed Testing procedure testing mean differences two functional populations. Functional data tested locally unadjusted adjusted p-value functions provided. unadjusted p-value function controls point-wise error rate. adjusted p-value function controls family-wise error rate asymptotically.

title: Two population Partition Closed Testing procedure — PCT2

title: Two population Partition Closed Testing procedure — PCT2
text: data1 Either numeric matrix object class fda::fd specifying data first sample. data provided within matrix, shape \(n_1 \times J\) contain row one \(n_1\) functions sample columns evaluation function uniform grid size \(J\). data2 Either numeric matrix object class fda::fd specifying data second sample. data provided within matrix, shape \(n_2 \times J\) contain row one \(n_2\) functions sample columns evaluation function uniform grid size \(J\). partition integer vector length \(J\) specifying membership point domain element partition. used must set correction argument set "PCT". mu Either numeric value numeric vector object class fda::fd specifying functional mean difference null hypothesis. mu constant, constant function used. mu numeric vector, must correspond evaluation mean difference function grid used evaluate data samples. Defaults 0. dx numeric value specifying discretization step grid used evaluate functional data provided objects class fda::fd. Defaults NULL, case default value 0.01 used corresponds grid size 100L. Unused functional data provided form matrices. B integer value specifying number iterations MC algorithm evaluate p-value permutation tests. Defaults 1000L. paired boolean value specifying whether paired test performed. Defaults FALSE. alternative string specifying type alternative hypothesis. Choices "two.sided", "less" "greater". Defaults "two.sided". n_perm integer value specifying number permutations permutation tests. Defaults 1000L.

title: Two population Partition Closed Testing procedure — PCT2
text: object class ftwosample containing following components: data: numeric matrix shape \(n \times J\) containing evaluation \(n = n_1 + n_2\) functions common uniform grid size \(p\). group_labels: integer vector size \(n = n_1 + n_2\) containing group membership function. mu: numeric vector shape \(J\) containing evaluation functional mean difference null hypothesis uniform grid used evaluate functional samples. unadjusted_pvalues: numeric vector size \(J\) containing evaluation unadjusted p-value function uniform grid used evaluate functional samples. adjusted_pvalues: numeric vector size \(J\) containing evaluation adjusted p-value functione uniform grid used evaluate functional samples. Optionally, list may contain following components: global_pvalue: numeric value containing global p-value. present correction argument set "Global". pvalue_matrix: numeric matrix shape \(p \times p\) containing p-values interval-wise tests. what: Local testing procedures for the functional analysis of variance — functional_anova_test
title: Local testing procedures for the functional analysis of variance — functional_anova_test
text: function implements local testing procedures testing mean differences multiple functional populations. Functional data tested locally unadjusted adjusted p-value functions provided. unadjusted p-value function controls point-wise error rate. adjusted p-value function can computed according following methods: global testing (controlling FWER weakly) interval-wise testing (controlling interval-wise error rate) threshold-wise testing (controlling FWER asymptotically) partition closed testing (controlling FWER partition) functional Benjamini Hochberg (controlling FDR)

what: Local testing procedures for functional-on-scalar linear models — functional_lm_test
title: Local testing procedures for functional-on-scalar linear models — functional_lm_test
text: function implements local testing procedures testing mean differences multiple functional populations. Functional data tested locally unadjusted adjusted p-value functions provided. unadjusted p-value function controls point-wise error rate. adjusted p-value function can computed according following methods: global testing (controlling FWER weakly) interval-wise testing (controlling interval-wise error rate) threshold-wise testing (controlling FWER asymptotically) partition closed testing (controlling FWER partition) functional Benjamini Hochberg (controlling FDR)

title: Local testing procedures for functional-on-scalar linear models — functional_lm_test
code: functional_lm_test(   formula,   correction,   dx = NULL,   B = 1000L,   method = c("residuals", "responses"),   recycle = TRUE,   stat = c("Integral", "Max") )

title: Local testing procedures for functional-on-scalar linear models — functional_lm_test
text: formula object class stats::formula (one can coerced class) specifying model fitted symbolic fashion. output variable (left-hand side) formula can either matrix dimension \(n \times J\) containing pointwise evaluations \(n\) functions grid \(J\) points, object class fda::fd. correction string specifying method used calculate adjusted p-value function. Choices either "Global" global testing, "IWT" interval-wise testing "TWT" threshold-wise testing. dx numeric value specifying discretization step grid used evaluate functional data provided objects class fda::fd. Defaults NULL, case default value 0.01 used corresponds grid size 100L. Unused functional data provided form matrices. B integer value specifying number iterations MC algorithm evaluate p-value permutation tests. Defaults 1000L. method string specifying method used calculate p-value permutation tests. Choices either "residuals" performs permutation residuals reduced model according Freedman Lane scheme "responses", performs permutation responses, according Manly scheme. Defaults "residuals". recycle boolean value specifying whether recycled version interval-wise testing procedure used. See Pini Vantini (2017) details. Defaults TRUE. stat string specifying test statistic used global test. Choices either "Integral", case statistic defined integral F-test statistic domain, "Max", case statistic defined maximum F-test statistic domain. Defaults  "Integral".

title: Local testing procedures for functional-on-scalar linear models — functional_lm_test
text: object class flm containing following components: call: matched call. design_matrix: design matrix functional--scalar linear model. unadjusted_pval_F: Evaluation grid unadjusted p-value function functional F-test. adjusted_pval_F: Evaluation grid adjusted p-value function functional F-test. unadjusted_pval_part: Evaluation grid unadjusted p-value function functional F-tests factor analysis variance (rows). adjusted_pval_part: Adjusted p-values functional F-tests factor analysis variance (rows) basis coefficient (columns). data_eval: Evaluation fine uniform grid functional data obtained basis expansion. coeff_regr_eval: Evaluation fine uniform grid functional regression coefficients. fitted_eval: Evaluation fine uniform grid fitted values functional regression. residuals_eval: Evaluation fine uniform grid residuals functional regression. R2_eval: Evaluation fine uniform grid functional R-squared regression. Optionally, list may contain following components: pval_matrix_F: Matrix dimensions c(p,p) p-values intervalwise F-tests. element \((,j)\) matrix pval_matrix contains p-value test interval indexed \((j,j+1,...,j+(p-))\); component present correction set "IWT". pval_matrix_part: Array dimensions c(L+1,p,p) p-values multivariate F-tests factors. element \((l,,j)\) array pval_matrix_part contains p-value joint NPC test factor l components \((j,j+1,...,j+(p-))\); component present correction set "IWT". Global_pval_F: Global p-value overall test F; component present correction set "Global". Global_pval_part: Global p-value test F involving factor separately; component present correction set "Global".

title: Local testing procedures for the functional two-sample test — functional_two_sample_test
text: function implements local testing procedures testing mean differences two functional populations. Functional data tested locally unadjusted adjusted p-value functions provided. unadjusted p-value function controls point-wise error rate. adjusted p-value function can computed according following methods: global testing (controlling FWER weakly) interval-wise testing (controlling interval-wise error rate) threshold-wise testing (controlling FWER asymptotically) partition closed testing (controlling FWER partition) functional Benjamini Hochberg (controlling FDR)

title: Local testing procedures for the functional two-sample test — functional_two_sample_test
code: functional_two_sample_test(   data1,   data2,   correction,   mu = 0,   dx = NULL,   B = 1000L,   paired = FALSE,   alternative = c("two.sided", "less", "greater"),   statistic = c("Integral", "Max", "Integral_std", "Max_std"),   recycle = TRUE,   partition = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Local testing procedures for the functional two-sample test — functional_two_sample_test
text: data1 Either numeric matrix object class fda::fd specifying data first sample. data provided within matrix, shape \(n_1 \times J\) contain row one \(n_1\) functions sample columns evaluation function uniform grid size \(J\). data2 Either numeric matrix object class fda::fd specifying data second sample. data provided within matrix, shape \(n_2 \times J\) contain row one \(n_2\) functions sample columns evaluation function uniform grid size \(J\). correction string specifying correction method perform local functional testing procedure adjust p-value function. Choices "Global, "IWT", "TWT", "PCT" "FDR". mu Either numeric value numeric vector object class fda::fd specifying functional mean difference null hypothesis. mu constant, constant function used. mu numeric vector, must correspond evaluation mean difference function grid used evaluate data samples. Defaults 0. dx numeric value specifying discretization step grid used evaluate functional data provided objects class fda::fd. Defaults NULL, case default value 0.01 used corresponds grid size 100L. Unused functional data provided form matrices. B integer value specifying number iterations MC algorithm evaluate p-value permutation tests. Defaults 1000L. paired boolean value specifying whether paired test performed. Defaults FALSE. alternative string specifying type alternative hypothesis. Choices "two.sided", "less" "greater". Defaults "two.sided". statistic string specifying test statistic use. Possible values : "Integral": Integral squared sample mean difference. "Max": Maximum squared sample mean difference. "Integral_std": Integral squared t-test statistic. "Max_std": Maximum squared t-test statistic. Defaults "Integral". recycle boolean value specifying whether recycled version interval-wise testing procedure used. See Pini Vantini (2017) details. Defaults TRUE. partition integer vector length \(J\) specifying membership point domain element partition. used must set correction argument set "PCT". verbose boolean value specifying whether print progress computation. Defaults FALSE.

title: Local testing procedures for the functional two-sample test — functional_two_sample_test
text: object class ftwosample containing following components: data: numeric matrix shape \(n \times J\) containing evaluation \(n = n_1 + n_2\) functions common uniform grid size \(p\). group_labels: integer vector size \(n = n_1 + n_2\) containing group membership function. mu: numeric vector shape \(J\) containing evaluation functional mean difference null hypothesis uniform grid used evaluate functional samples. unadjusted_pvalues: numeric vector size \(J\) containing evaluation unadjusted p-value function uniform grid used evaluate functional samples. adjusted_pvalues: numeric vector size \(J\) containing evaluation adjusted p-value functione uniform grid used evaluate functional samples. Optionally, list may contain following components: global_pvalue: numeric value containing global p-value. present correction argument set "Global". pvalue_matrix: numeric matrix shape \(p \times p\) containing p-values interval-wise tests. Element \(, j\) contains p-value test performed interval indexed \(j, j+1 , \dots, j+(p-)\). present correction argument set "IWT".
